pages.raw_text:
Several unions interested in the
building Of the Home Telephone
lines in this city met Sunday and
agreed to place the Home company
and the Empire Electric Construc-
ton Co. on the unfair list until
the concern kept its agreement to
employ union men at union wages
In its construction work. They al
so resolved to refuse to permit
members to patronize any company
that put Home telephone in its
place Of business.


A committee representing these
union waited upon the central body
last night and asked that the board
OF public works compel the Home
people to submit all their plans for
conduits and other work to the
board before commencing work In
compliance with the city ordk
nances. The central body complied
with the request and It's commit
tee was informed today that the
company had already been asked to
submit It's plans TO the board.


1t Is believed that the trouble
between the unions and the Home
Telephone Co. will be settled at az,
early date. A settlement has. it Ia
reported, been made in Portland
and ether cities. In Denver the
company posted Il $4,000 bond to
keep Its compact with the unions,
